comments.text: I feel for the Hawaiian People! Such a violent history of people coming trying to dominate their culture and home. Like the English colonizing and brutalizing countries such as India, Australia.  Such power and control has caused so much damage. Governments and Monarch’s wanting to process   what doesn’t belong to them.  Now Greenland!!!  They will never get it.  Like Children throwing tantrums to get what they want at the cost of others.  I respect the Hawaiian people for all they have endured. ☮️

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for your thoughtful comment. One of our goals was to encourage viewers to learn more about Hawaii's history as well as its natural beauty. We appreciate you sharing your perspective. ❤

comments.author: @samyelshamy214
comments.text: No. "Aloha" means "Allah" in Arabic grammar ("Allaho"), and it seems the pronunciation changed over time to "Aloha". Which means the native Hawaiians were Muslims. The first one to name those who followed the original religion of our father Adam "Muslims," was Prophet Ibrahim,  [Quran 22-78].

comments.author: @PMMcDaniel
comments.text: Too bad the AI narrator butchered 98-99% of the Hawaiian words.  Just made me cringe and or laugh every time "he" mispronounced the names, places and foods I love on each Island.  Other than that, beautiful footage.

replies.author: @TraceTheGlobe
replies.text: Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts. We appreciate your honesty and understand your frustration with the pronunciation. We'll definitely put more effort into getting local names right in future videos. We're glad you still enjoyed the visuals. ❤

comments.text: I thank you so much for this video of Hawaii. I know there where scens that you claim it was the mountains of Kauai, but it was the Koolau mountain range on Oahu. But your content was good, you covered things from the history to current.
